methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 15 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Defense & Security. Range: $500M - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Anduril Industries
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (SPAC), post-money $78B+

data.narrative:
True Anomaly, a defense and security technology company at the SPAC stage, is valued at $2.2B against $600M in total raised capital. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 15 same-sector peers across similar valuation tiers, comprising 2 public companies and 8 private firms. Key comparables include Shield AI ($5.6B, Series G), Flock Safety ($7.5B, Series E), Saronic ($9.2B, Series D), Chaos Industries ($4.5B, Series D), and Skydio ($2.2B, Series E).

The peer-median methodology, the primary applicable triangulation approach given available data, yields an implied valuation of $1.5B based on the median of the 15-company peer set. This method establishes a floor at $500M and ceiling at $9.2B across the comparable universe. The single-method triangulation produces a range of $1.3B to $1.7B, with a median of $1.5B.

The current $2.2B valuation sits approximately 47% above the peer median, positioning True Anomaly at a meaningful premium within its sector cohort. Key uncertainties limiting confidence include reliance on a single valuation methodology and limited visibility into the underlying revenue, growth rate, and profitability profiles of both the target and peer set. Stage-specific valuation dynamics at the SPAC juncture and relative competitive positioning would benefit from additional transaction multiples or DCF analysis to strengthen the triangulation.
